_note: Feature tables for the steampunk classes. n = feature name, d = one-line rules text the DM treats as authoritative.

1.d: Casts wizard-list spells as assembled schematics using INT; the toolbelt is the focus.
2/rest, 1 minute of work restores 1d8 + INT HP to a construct, machine, or armored ally.

2.d: After studying a machine or mechanism for a minute, you can describe, operate, or sabotage it competently.

5.d: 1/rest, cast a 1st-level schematic without expending a slot.

6.d: A small brass automaton assists you: it carries, fetches, and grants advantage on one check per rest.

7.d: +1 AC while wearing armor you have tuned (any armor after a long rest).

9.d: 1/rest, adjacent creatures save (DEX) or take 3d8 fire and are pushed 10 ft when you vent your armor.

10.d: Your schematics ignore half cover and treat three-quarters cover as half.

11.d: Field Repair restores 2d8 + INT and has 4 uses per rest.

13.d: 1/long rest, regain expended slot levels equal to your INT modifier.

14.d: Choose one 3rd-level schematic; cast it 1/long rest without a slot.

15.d: Advantage on CON saves to maintain concentration on schematics.

17.d: 1/rest, take the calculated answer: treat one INT-based check as a natural 20.

18.d: Overpressure Valve works for schematics up to 3rd level, twice per rest.

20.d: 1/long rest, cast one schematic of 6th level or lower without a slot; constructs and machines cannot resist it.

3.d: Your familiar gains a sting (1d8 + INT piercing, 30 ft) and HP equal to 3 x your level.
Command your familiar as a bonus action.

7.d: 1/rest, loose a box of whirring gears: a 15-ft cube becomes difficult terrain and deals 2d6 slashing to creatures entering it, for 1 minute.

11.d: Build a second familiar; both act on your command.

15.d: 1/long rest, your familiars merge into a brass juggernaut for 10 minutes (double HP, 2d10 + INT slam, you may ride it).

1.d: Advantage on checks to climb, balance, and keep footing; falling damage you take is halved.
1/turn, add +1d6 damage to an attack made after moving at least 15 ft in a straight or swinging line.

2.d: Climbing costs no extra movement; Dash as a bonus action.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: A launcher and line: swing or pull yourself 30 ft as part of your movement, INT-mod uses per rest.

7.d: DEX saves for half damage deal none on a success, half on a failure.

9.d: Reaction when missed in melee: riposte with one weapon attack.

10.d: You read wind and weather a day ahead and are never surprised by conditions aloft.

11.d: Daring Maneuver rises to +2d6 and also applies to ranged attacks made while airborne or elevated.

13.d: 1/rest, when combat starts, immediately move your speed and make one attack.

14.d: Immune to falling damage while conscious; opportunity attacks against you have disadvantage.

15.d: Grapnel Line can also disarm or trip (contested by STR) at 30 ft.

17.d: Your critical hits with one-handed weapons knock the target prone or disarm it (your choice).

18.d: Your speed increases by 10 ft; difficult terrain never slows you.

20.d: When you roll initiative, gain a full extra turn before the first round begins.

3.d: When you attack with a melee weapon, make one firearm attack as a bonus action.
Advantage on Intimidation and Persuasion aboard ships and in ports.

7.d: Your firearm attacks ignore half cover; reloading never costs you an attack.

11.d: 1/rest, fire at up to three targets in a 15-ft cone with a single attack roll each.

15.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute your Daring Maneuver applies to every attack and enemies who see you save (WIS) or are frightened.

1.d: Casts wizard-list spells as brewed formulae using INT; damaging formulae may be hurled as flasks.
A prepared stone: 1/rest, add +1d4 to one formula's damage or healing.

3.d: You can identify any potion, poison, or chemical by smell and taste safely.

5.d: Brew 2 draughts per long rest: drinker regains 2d4 + INT HP as a bonus action.

6.d: Resistance to acid and poison damage.

7.d: 1/rest, a stinking cloud fills a 10-ft radius for 1 minute: creatures inside save (CON) or retch, losing their action.

9.d: Catalyst Stone adds +1d8 and has 3 uses per rest.

10.d: 10 minutes converts up to 1 cubic foot of one common material to another (wood to iron, water to acid).

11.d: 1/long rest, cast a formula of 3rd level or lower without expending a slot.

13.d: Emergency Draughts also cure one poison or disease and grant 1 hour of poison immunity.

14.d: Your damaging formulae may splash: one creature adjacent to the target takes your INT mod in the same damage type.

15.d: Catalyst Stone can instead reroll all of a formula's dice, keeping the better total.

17.d: 1/long rest, brew a greater elixir: drinker gains advantage on everything for 1 minute.

18.d: Your acid formulae ignore resistance and melt nonmagical barriers of 1 ft thickness or less.

20.d: 1/long rest, cast one formula of 7th level or lower without a slot; its dice are maximized.

2.d: 1/turn, a damaging formula deals +1d6 acid; you may change any formula's damage type to acid.
Your acid damage reduces a hit target's AC by 1 until your next turn (not cumulative).

6.d: Reaction when hit in melee: the attacker takes 2d6 acid as an ampoule bursts, INT-mod uses per long rest.

10.d: 1/long rest, a 20-ft radius acid fog for 1 minute: 3d6 acid at the start of each creature's turn inside (CON save halves).

14.d: Your acid formulae treat immunity as resistance; Etching Acid stacks to -3 AC.

1.d: Pick two gadgets (grapnel, smoke bomb, arc lamp, ear trumpet, skeleton key, pocket shield); each usable INT-mod times per rest with sensible effects.
1/rest, a gadget or machine you hold works at double effect for 1 minute.

2.d: Draw, swap, or deploy gadgets as a bonus action.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: Learn a third gadget; gadget saves against your effects use 8 + proficiency + INT.

7.d: Your jump distance triples; ignore the first 20 ft of any fall.

9.d: Your smoke bombs fill a 15-ft radius and you see through your own smoke.

10.d: Learn a fourth gadget; Overclock has 2 uses per rest.

11.d: 1/rest, a touched machine, trap, or construct malfunctions at the worst moment for its owner (DM adjudicates).

13.d: When an effect would destroy or disable a held gadget, it survives; 1/long rest, ignore being disarmed.

15.d: Use two different gadgets with the same bonus action.

17.d: Design one custom gadget with the DM; it is yours alone and recharges per short rest.

18.d: All gadget uses recharge on a short rest.

20.d: 1/long rest, declare you prepared exactly the right device for this situation; the DM makes it work once.

3.d: Add +1d4 to attack rolls made with gadget-augmented weapons (tech blades and firearms you have tuned).
Advantage on checks to understand or copy unfamiliar technology.

7.d: 1/rest, your tuned weapon deals +2d8 lightning on one hit; the target cannot take reactions until its next turn.

11.d: Once per turn, make one extra firearm attack as part of the Attack action.

15.d: Your tuned weapons count as +2 magical weapons and their critical range improves to 19-20.

1.d: Casts sorcerer-list spells as aetheric arts using CHA; sparks visibly arc across you when you cast.
1/rest, a creature that touches or melee-hits you takes 1d8 lightning.

2.d: Detect active machines, aether lines, and charged creatures within 30 ft.

5.d: 2/rest, cast a cantrip as a bonus action after casting a leveled art.

7.d: Your touch runs any machine without fuel for 1 hour, CHA-mod uses per long rest.

9.d: 1/long rest, absorb a lightning or aether effect targeting you: negate it and regain a 1st-level slot.

11.d: 1/rest, turn to crackling energy and move 30 ft through creatures and objects as a bonus action.

13.d: 1/long rest, a damaging art rolls maximum dice; you take 1d10 lightning backlash.

15.d: 1/long rest, regain expended slot levels equal to your CHA modifier.

17.d: Immunity to lightning damage; Galvanic Discharge is always active.

18.d: Machines and constructs have disadvantage on saves against your arts.

20.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute your arts of 3rd level or lower cost no slots.

1.d: 1/turn, add your CHA modifier as lightning damage to a damaging art.

6.d: 1/rest, a single-target lightning art jumps to a second target within 15 ft for half damage.

10.d: 1/long rest, a whirling storm shield for 1 minute: +2 AC and melee attackers take 2d8 lightning.

14.d: Your lightning arts ignore resistance and treat immunity as resistance.

1.d: Your tuned armor grants +1 AC and immunity to being pushed while conscious.
1/turn, +1d6 bludgeoning on a melee hit as the pistons slam.

2.d: Bonus action: build pressure. Your next Piston Strike this turn deals +2d6 instead.

5.d: Attack twice when taking the Attack action.

6.d: 1/rest, adjacent creatures save (CON) or are blinded by scalding mist until your next turn.

7.d: When you move 15+ ft straight toward a creature and hit it, it saves (STR) or is knocked prone.

9.d: Advantage on saves against poison gas and drowning; the armor is airtight for 10 minutes.

10.d: Boiler Plate grants +2 AC; resistance to fire damage.

11.d: Attack three times when taking the Attack action.

13.d: 1/rest, when reduced below half HP, vent: attackers within 10 ft take 3d8 fire (DEX save halves).

14.d: Your speed cannot be reduced; difficult terrain never slows you while armored.

15.d: Piston Strike applies to every melee hit, not once per turn.

17.d: Critical hits against you become normal hits.

18.d: 1/long rest, double your speed and gain an extra action each turn for 1 minute; take 3d10 fire when it ends.

20.d: While conscious, regain 5 HP at the start of your turn if below half HP; Head of Steam is free once per turn.

3.d: Enemies within your reach treat the area as difficult terrain.
Adjacent allies gain +1 AC while you hold a shield.

7.d: Reaction: swap places with an adjacent ally targeted by an attack; you become the target.

11.d: Allies behind you have three-quarters cover against ranged attacks.

15.d: 1/long rest, for 1 minute you and adjacent allies have resistance to all damage.
